var pmb = {"page_per_post":1,"max_image_size":"800"};// This is some Prince HTML-to-PDF converter Javascript. It's not executed by the browser, but during the // Prince HTML-to-PDF conversion process. See https://www.princexml.com/doc/javascript/ // turn on box tracking API Prince.trackBoxes = true; // once the first pass of rendering is finished, let's make the "pmb-dynamic-resize" images take up the rest of the // page they're on. Prince will then need to re-render. Prince.registerPostLayoutFunc(function() { pmb_continue_image_resizing(); pmb_extend_to_bottom(); }); /** * Resizes images in blocks with CSS class "pmb-dynamic-resize". * Gutenberg image blocks with no alignment: the top-level block has the class and is the figure. * But if they're floating, the top-level block is a div which contains the figure (which floats). * The image's initial height effectively becomes the minimum height. The maximum height */ function pmb_continue_image_resizing(){ var resized_something = false; // To make this more efficient, grab the first image from each section followed by a pagebreak if(pmb.page_per_post){ var dynamic_resize_blocks = document.getElementsByClassName('pmb-section'); for(var i=0; i<dynamic_resize_blocks.length; i++){ var resized_element = pmb_resize_an_image_inside(dynamic_resize_blocks[i]); if(resized_element){ resized_something = true; } } } else { if(pmb_resize_an_image_inside(document)){ resized_something = true; } } if(resized_something){ Prince.registerPostLayoutFunc(pmb_continue_image_resizing); } } /** * Grabs a "pmb-dynamic-resize" element inside here and resizes it and returns it. * (If none are found, returns null) * @param element * @return boolean */ function pmb_resize_an_image_inside(element){ var dynamic_resize_blocks = element.getElementsByClassName("pmb-dynamic-resize"); // just grab one block at a time because how the first image is resized will affect the subsequentn ones // and subsequent ones' telemetry is only updated after re-rendering. So do one, then get Prince to re-render, then // do another... etc. var a_dynamic_resize_block = dynamic_resize_blocks[0]; if(typeof a_dynamic_resize_block === 'undefined'){ return null; } // when images are floating, the block had a div (with no height) because its contents are floating // in that case we want to resize the figure inside the block. So check if there's a figure inside it var figure_is_floating = true; var figure_to_resize = a_dynamic_resize_block.getElementsByTagName("figure")[0]; if( typeof figure_to_resize === 'undefined'){ // There's no figure inside it. The figure is the top-level element in the block. figure_to_resize = a_dynamic_resize_block; figure_is_floating = false; } // For floating images we need to also set the block's width (I can't figure out how to get CSS to set the width automatically) // so for that we need to figure out how much the image inside the figure got resized (non-trivial if there's a caption). var figure_image = figure_to_resize.getElementsByTagName('img')[0]; // determine the caption's height var figure_caption = figure_to_resize.getElementsByTagName('figcaption')[0]; var caption_height = 0; if(typeof(figure_caption) !== 'undefined'){ var caption_box = figure_caption.getPrinceBoxes()[0]; caption_height = caption_box.marginTop + caption_box.h + caption_box.marginBottom; } // If we can't find an image to resize, there's nothing to resize (which is weird but somehow happens?) if(typeof(figure_image) !== 'undefined') { var figure_image_box = figure_image.getPrinceBoxes()[0]; var figure_image_height = figure_image_box.h; var figure_box = figure_to_resize.getPrinceBoxes()[0]; var page_box = PDF.pages[figure_box.pageNum - 1]; // don't forget to take the footnote height into account var footnotes_height = 0; for (var index in page_box['children']) { var box_on_page = page_box['children'][index]; if (box_on_page['type'] === 'FOOTNOTES') { footnotes_height = box_on_page['h']; } } var max_allowable_height = pmb_px_to_pts(pmb.max_image_size); // page_box.y is the distance from the top of the page to the bottom margin; // page_box.h is the distance from the bottom margin to the top margin // figure_box.y is the distance from the top of the page to the bottom-left corner of the figure // see https://www.princexml.com/forum/post/23543/attachment/img-fill.html var top_margin = page_box.y - page_box.h; var remaining_vertical_space = figure_box.y - top_margin - 10 - footnotes_height; var max_height_because_of_max_width = page_box.w * figure_image_box.h / figure_image_box.w + caption_height; // also gather the maximum heights from the original image var max_height_from_resolution_y_of_image = 100000; if('height' in figure_image.attributes){ max_height_from_resolution_y_of_image = pmb_px_to_pts(figure_image.attributes['height'].value); } // resolution_height px max_height pts // ------------------ = -------------- => max_height = max_width pts * original_height px / original_width px // resolution_width px max_width pts var max_height_from_resolution_x_of_image = 100000; if('width' in figure_image.attributes && 'height' in figure_image.attributes){ max_height_from_resolution_x_of_image = (page_box.w * figure_image.attributes['height'].value / figure_image.attributes['width'].value) + caption_height; } Log.info('IMG:' + figure_image.attributes['src'].value); Log.info(' page width:' + page_box.w); Log.info(' pmb.max_image_size' + pmb.max_image_size); Log.info(' remaining_vertical_space ' + remaining_vertical_space + '(distance to bottom margin ' + page_box.y + ', figure bottom at ' + figure_box.y + ')'); Log.info(' max_height_because_of_max_width' + max_height_because_of_max_width); Log.info(' max_height_from_resolution_y_of_image' + max_height_from_resolution_y_of_image); Log.info(' max_height_from_resolution_x_of_image' + max_height_from_resolution_x_of_image); Log.info(' caption height ' + caption_height); // put a limit on how big the image can be // use the design's maximum image size, which was passed from PHP var new_figure_height = Math.min( max_allowable_height, remaining_vertical_space, max_height_because_of_max_width, max_height_from_resolution_y_of_image, max_height_from_resolution_x_of_image ); Log.info('New figure size is ' + new_figure_height); var max_class = 'pmb-dynamic-resize-limited-by-unknown'; switch(new_figure_height){ case max_allowable_height: max_class = 'pmb-dynamic-resize-limited-by-max_allowable_height'; break; case remaining_vertical_space: max_class = 'pmb-dynamic-resize-limited-by-remaining_vertical_space'; break; case max_height_because_of_max_width: max_class = 'pmb-dynamic-resize-limited-by-max_height_because_of_max_width'; break; case max_height_from_resolution_y_of_image: max_class = 'pmb-dynamic-resize-limited-by-max_height_from_resolution_y_of_image'; break; case max_height_from_resolution_x_of_image: max_class = 'pmb-dynamic-resize-limited-by-max_height_from_resolution_x_of_image'; break; } Log.info('max height css class:'+ max_class); // Resize the block figure_to_resize.style.height = new_figure_height + "pt"; if (figure_is_floating) { // Used some grade 12 math to figure out this equation. var new_image_height = new_figure_height - figure_box.h + figure_image_height; var resize_ratio = new_image_height / figure_image_height; figure_to_resize.style.width = (figure_box.w * resize_ratio) + 'pt'; } } // Change the class so we know we don't try to resize this block again a_dynamic_resize_block.className = a_dynamic_resize_block.className.replace(/pmb-dynamic-resize/g, 'pmb-dynamic-resized') + ' ' + max_class; return a_dynamic_resize_block; } function pmb_extend_to_bottom(){ Log.info('pmb_extend_to_bottom'); // find all elements that should extend to bottom var dynamic_resize_elements = document.getElementsByClassName('pmb-fill-remaining-height'); if(dynamic_resize_elements.length){ Log.info('found something to resize'); // find their distance to the bottom of the page var element = dynamic_resize_elements[0]; var element_box = element.getPrinceBoxes()[0]; var page_box = PDF.pages[element_box.pageNum - 1]; Log.info('element to resize'); pmb_print_props(element_box, 'element to resize box'); pmb_print_props(page_box, 'page box'); var remaining_vertical_space = element_box.y - (page_box.y - page_box.h) - 50; Log.info('resize to ' + remaining_vertical_space); // make the element fill that vertical height element.style.height = remaining_vertical_space + "pt"; // remember not to do this one again element.className = element.className.replace(/pmb-fill-remaining-height/g, 'pmb-filled-remaining-height'); // redraw and look again Prince.registerPostLayoutFunc(pmb_extend_to_bottom); } else { Log.info('nothing more to do'); } } /** * From https://www.princexml.com/doc/cookbook/#how-and-where-is-my-box * @param pixels * @returns {number} */ function pmb_px_to_pts(pixels){ return pixels * (72 / 96); } /** * A debugging function, especially useful for figuring out what's on these "box" objects * @param obj * @param label */ function pmb_print_props(obj, label){ Log.info(label); for(var prop in obj){ var val = obj[prop]; Log.info(prop + ':' + val); } }
Back

Table of Contents

30-Year-Old Reno Homes

June 27, 2025 Structural Repairs

How to Add an ADU in Nevada Without Breaking the Bank (2025 Guide)

30-Year-Old Reno Homes

Reno Home Repairs for 30-Year-Old Houses – Structural Alert

A 30-year-old home in Northern Nevada is likely dealing with real structural degradation—from shifting foundations to failing insulation, rusted HVACs, and even unsafe wiring. Whether your house was built in the 90s or before, these are the problems most homeowners miss until it’s too late:

Ceiling Texture Failure

Popcorn ceilings sag and detach in homes built pre-1995 as rooflines shift and past leaks degrade gypsum board.

Popcorn ceilings sag and detach in homes built pre-1995 as rooflines shift and past leaks degrade gypsum board.

Get a Quote

Foundation Movement

Homes in Hidden Valley show diagonal drywall cracks and stuck doors due to long-term soil expansion shifting slabs.

Homes in Hidden Valley show diagonal drywall cracks and stuck doors due to long-term soil expansion shifting slabs.

Get a Quote

Rodent Entry Points

Rodents access crawlspaces via gaps in aging mesh barriers, bringing debris and causing insulation movement.

Rodents access crawlspaces via gaps in aging mesh barriers, bringing debris and causing insulation movement.

Get a Quote

Rusty Furnace Damage

Rust inside furnaces from 90s humidifiers and hard water accelerates heat exchanger failure and vent pipe damage.

Rust inside furnaces from 90s humidifiers and hard water accelerates heat exchanger failure and vent pipe damage.

Get a Quote

Unsafe Deck Framing

Decks built pre-2000 often lack proper joist spacing; snow load causes sagging or full structural separation.

Decks built pre-2000 often lack proper joist spacing; snow load causes sagging or full structural separation.

Get a Quote

Compacted Attic Insulation

Attics insulated with 1990s batts lose volume and R-value; compacted insulation no longer protects from heat loss.

Attics insulated with 1990s batts lose volume and R-value; compacted insulation no longer protects from heat loss.

Get a Quote

Galvanized Pipe Corrosion

Galvanized pipes corrode inside over time, causing leaks in wall cavities and rusty water from older fittings.

Galvanized pipes corrode inside over time, causing leaks in wall cavities and rusty water from older fittings.

Get a Quote

Aluminum Wiring Risk

Aluminum wires overheat in outlets, switches, and fixtures—especially under new dimmers or smart devices today.

Aluminum wires overheat in outlets, switches, and fixtures—especially under new dimmers or smart devices today.

Get a Quote
[wpforms id=”308″ title=”false” description=”false”]

20-Year-Old Reno Homes

June 27, 2025 Structural Repairs

20-Year-Old Reno Homes

Reno Home Repairs for 20-Year-Old Houses – Time to Act

At 20 years old, Reno homes often face hidden wear that homeowners only notice after damage is done. From outdated HVAC systems to sagging decks and cracked tile showers, issues are often caused by aging materials and original construction shortcuts. If your home was built in the early 2000s, pay close attention to the following:

10-Year-Old Reno Homes

30-Year-Old Reno Homes

Attic Mold Growth

Attic plywood molds from warm air trapped by poor vents, often seen in homes built before 2005 in South Reno.

Attic plywood molds from warm air trapped by poor vents, often seen in homes built before 2005 in South Reno.

Get a Quote

Frame Separation

Vinyl or aluminum window frames detach from sills as shifting slabs and UV exposure weaken anchors over time. like last one

Vinyl or aluminum window frames detach from sills as shifting slabs and UV exposure weaken anchors over time. like last one

Get a Quote

Sagging Kitchen Floors

Kitchen floors soften under laminate due to long-term subfloor moisture or gaps in vapor barriers below sinks.

Kitchen floors soften under laminate due to long-term subfloor moisture or gaps in vapor barriers below sinks.

Get a Quote

Cracked Shower Walls

Tile walls around showers crack due to grout decay, allowing water to rot wallboard behind second-floor baths.

Tile walls around showers crack due to grout decay, allowing water to rot wallboard behind second-floor baths.

Get a Quote

Failing HVAC Systems

Furnaces over 18 years old lose efficiency and risk failure; especially common in Toll Brothers subdivisions.

Furnaces over 18 years old lose efficiency and risk failure; especially common in Toll Brothers subdivisions.

Get a Quote

Faulty Breaker Panels

Breaker boxes from early 2000s lack GFCI/arc fault protection and may overheat from modern appliance usage.

Breaker boxes from early 2000s lack GFCI/arc fault protection and may overheat from modern appliance usage.

Get a Quote

Shifting Porch Columns

Stucco porch columns in Double Diamond shift from their bases as builder footings settle and shrink unevenly.

Stucco porch columns in Double Diamond shift from their bases as builder footings settle and shrink unevenly.

Get a Quote

Basement Water Intrusion

Basements near Damonte Ranch flood in spring when clay soil expands and groundwater rises post-snowmelt.

Basements near Damonte Ranch flood in spring when clay soil expands and groundwater rises post-snowmelt.

Get a Quote
[wpforms id=”308″ title=”false” description=”false”]

What Reno Homeowners Overlook in 10 year Old Homes

June 27, 2025 Structural Repairs

10-Year-Old Reno Homes

Reno Home Repairs for 10-Year-Old Houses – What You’re Missing

A 10-year-old home in Reno may still look modern, but early structural and environmental stress is already underway. The high desert climate, UV intensity, and expansive soils begin showing their effects around this age. Here are the most overlooked problems we fix in newer homes across Reno, Spanish Springs, and Cold Springs:

20-Year-Old Reno Homes

30-Year-Old Reno Homes

Soil-Related Cracking

Expansive clay soil causes drywall cracks in Spanish Springs and South Reno as early foundation settling begins

Expansive clay soil causes drywall cracks in Spanish Springs and South Reno as early foundation settling begins

Get a Quote

Sun Damage on Siding

Reno’s sun fades siding and paint faster on west walls, drying materials and causing early surface brittleness.

Reno’s sun fades siding and paint faster on west walls, drying materials and causing early surface brittleness.

Get a Quote

Leaky Attic Ducts

Attic ducts in newer Reno homes leak cold air and overwork AC units, raising utility costs during dry summers.

Attic ducts in newer Reno homes leak cold air and overwork AC units, raising utility costs during dry summers.

Get a Quote

Driveway Erosion

Freeze-thaw erosion wears down unsealed driveways in high desert zones, leading to scaling and cracking early.

Freeze-thaw erosion wears down unsealed driveways in high desert zones, leading to scaling and cracking early.

Get a Quote

Deck Splintering

Decks fade fast in Reno’s UV-heavy climate; untreated boards splinter from heat and dry air within 8–10 years.

Decks fade fast in Reno’s UV-heavy climate; untreated boards splinter from heat and dry air within 8–10 years.

Get a Quote

Sprinkler Line Leaks

Irrigation lines not winterized correctly leak into crawlspaces or foundations after freeze-thaw soil movement.

Irrigation lines not winterized correctly leak into crawlspaces or foundations after freeze-thaw soil movement.

Get a Quote

Stucco Hairline Cracks

Stucco cracking in Sparks and Cold Springs can indicate internal moisture issues or improper expansion joints.

Stucco cracking in Sparks and Cold Springs can indicate internal moisture issues or improper expansion joints.

Get a Quote

Blocked Attic Vents

Blocked attic baffles trap heat and moisture, contributing to slow mold buildup and degraded insulation above.

Decks fade fast in Reno’s UV-heavy climate; untreated boards splinter from heat and dry air within 8–10 years.

Get a Quote

Why More Reno Homeowners Trust BRE Builders for Structural Repairs

June 4, 2025 Structural Repairs

What Makes BRE Builders the Structural Repair Experts in Reno?

BRE Builders, a division of Blue Reef Enterprises, is known for structural repairs that last—especially in Northern Nevada’s harsh soil and weather conditions.

We repair the core of your home’s –  Reno-specific experience. 

We repair the core of your home’s –  Reno-specific experience. 

We repair the core of your home’s –  Reno-specific experience. 

We repair the core of your home’s –  Reno-specific experience. 

Common Structural Problems We Fix:

Foundation cracks and bowing walls
Sagging floors and uneven support beams
Damaged joists or subflooring from water/mold
Load-bearing beam reinforcement
Settling piers and failing crawlspaces

Explore All Structural Services

Top Warning Signs Your Home May Need Structural Repair

Cracks in ceilings or drywall (especially near corners)

Sloping or bouncy floors

Doors or windows that suddenly stick

Gaps between walls and flooring

Crawlspace smells, visible rot, or rusted posts

Need Urgent Repairs or Ongoing Maintenance?

Reach out now

Top 5 Mistakes Reno Homeowners Make. 

Top 5 Mistakes Reno Homeowners Make. 

Ignoring small cracks that grow
DIY jack post repairs without structural knowledge
Hiring a handyman for load-bearing issues
Ignoring drainage problems causing soil erosion
Delaying inspections when damage is active

Reno Kitchen Remodeling: Top Trends & Investment Tips for 2025

June 2, 2025 Kitchen Remodeling

Building Beautiful Reno Nevada Kitchens Bathrooms Homes

Are you a Reno homeowner dreaming of a kitchen that’s both beautiful and functional? A kitchen remodel can significantly enhance your lifestyle and boost your home’s value. But with so many options available, how do you choose the best approach for your Reno home and budget? This blog post will guide you through the top kitchen remodeling trends in Reno for 2025 and offer valuable investment tips to maximize your return.

Reno Kitchen Remodeling Trends for 2025

Embracing Natural Warmth:

Expect to see an increase in natural materials like wood cabinets, stone countertops (quartz, granite), and earthy tones. These elements create a warm and inviting atmosphere, especially appealing in the Reno climate.

The Rise of Smart Kitchens:

Technology is playing a bigger role, with homeowners integrating smart appliances, touchless faucets, and voice-activated lighting. This trend emphasizes efficiency and convenience.

Backsplashes as Focal Points:

Backsplashes are becoming a focal point, with unique tile patterns, textured materials, and pops of color adding personality and visual interest.

Maximized Storage Solutions:

Clever storage solutions, such as pull-out drawers, pantry organizers, and hidden charging stations, are key to keeping kitchens clutter-free and efficient.

The Ever-Popular Functional Island:

Kitchen islands continue to be popular, offering extra counter space, seating, and storage. Consider incorporating a cooktop or sink into your island for added functionality.

Smart Kitchen Remodeling Investments for Reno Homes

Invest in Quality Countertops & Cabinets:

These are high-impact features that significantly affect both aesthetics and functionality. Choose durable and stylish options that appeal to a broad range of buyers.

Upgrade to Energy-Efficient Appliances:

Upgrading to energy-efficient models reduces utility costs and appeals to eco-conscious buyers, adding value to your home.

Prioritize Updated Lighting:

A well-lit kitchen is essential. Consider a combination of ambient, task, and accent lighting to brighten up the space and highlight your design features.

Optimize with Functional Layouts:

A good layout improves workflow and makes the kitchen more enjoyable to use. If your current layout isn’t working, consider consulting with a kitchen designer to optimize the space.

Ventilation is Key:

Proper ventilation is crucial for removing cooking odors and improving indoor air quality. Invest in a quality range hood to maintain a healthy and comfortable kitchen environment.

BRE Builders: Your Reno Kitchen Remodeling Experts

BRE Builders is a trusted kitchen remodeling company in Reno, specializing in creating beautiful, functional, and valuable spaces. Contact BRE Builders for a free consultation to design and build a kitchen that fits your style, budget, and needs.

Investing in a kitchen remodel can boost a Reno home’s value and create a space for years to come. By focusing on trends and making smart investments, it’s possible to maximize return and create a dream kitchen that is both beautiful and functional. Contact BRE Builders today to discuss kitchen remodeling goals.

Is Your Kitchen Ruining Your Property Value? Fix These 5 Things First

May 27, 2025 Kitchen Remodeling

We Specialize In Affordable Kitchen Remodeling Cabinet Refacing & Upgrades Countertop Replacement Lighting & Electrical Improvements Smart Layout Fixes Flooring That Boosts Resale Value Quick Budget-Friendly Kitchen Fixes “That Add Real Home Value”

Outdated Cabinets and Hardware

Old, yellowed cabinets or brass knobs can date your kitchen by decades.

Why It Matters: First impressions count — dated cabinets instantly lower perceived value.

Fix It: Reface, repaint, or update hardware for a fast, high-ROI upgrade.

Poor Kitchen Lighting

Dim, uneven lighting makes even clean kitchens feel dingy.

Why It Matters: Lighting affects mood, function, and how buyers view your space.

Fix It: Add task and accent lighting, and replace bulbs with warm, energy-efficient LEDs.

Worn or Cracked Countertops


Outdated or damaged surfaces make kitchens feel neglected.

Why It Matters: Countertops are focal points — cracks and burns are red flags.

Fix It: Upgrade to quartz or affordable butcher block, or use resurfacing as a budget fix.

Inefficient Layout or Traffic Flow


Tight walkways, poor appliance placement, or blocked access frustrate users.

Why It Matters: A beautiful kitchen that’s hard to use is a dealbreaker.

Fix It: Reconfigure layout, add prep zones, or remove cluttered uppers.

Flooring That Dates the Whole House

Vinyl, old tile, or chipped laminate can drag down your home’s appeal.

Why It Matters: Buyers associate flooring with home quality — bad floors = low offers.

Fix It: Use luxury vinyl plank (LVP) for a modern, durable, and budget-friendly solution.

Frequently Asked Questions – BRE Builders | Reno Home Remodeling, ADUs & Repairs

May 26, 2025

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) – BRE Builders

Your Reno-Based Experts in Structural Repairs, Remodeling, Deck Restoration & More

Who is BRE Builders?

BRE Builders is a licensed, locally owned and operated home improvement contractor serving Reno, Sparks, Carson City, and surrounding areas. We specialize in structural repairs, custom remodeling, ADUs, deck restoration, waterproofing, and more. We deliver reliable, code-compliant, and long-lasting solutions.

What areas do you serve?

We proudly serve:

  • Reno, NV

  • Sparks, NV

  • Carson City, NV

  • Lake Tahoe & Truckee, CA

  • Carson Valley & surrounding communities

Need service outside these areas? Contact us to discuss.

What services do you provide?

We offer:

  • Structural repairs & home stabilization

  • Deck safety inspections & full rebuilds

  • Waterproofing, custom railings & finishes

  • Kitchen & bathroom remodels

  • New home additions & ADU construction

  • Concrete slab work and framing

  • Office & commercial renovations

Explore our full services here.

Do you handle permit and code compliance?

Yes. BRE Builders manages the entire permitting process and ensures all projects meet local Nevada and California building codes. We never cut corners.

Do you offer free estimates?

Absolutely. We provide free, no-obligation estimates after a site visit. Get started by filling out our project form or calling us directly.

How soon can you start my project?

Project timelines vary based on season and project scope. Once a contract is signed and permits (if any) are secured, we typically begin within 1–2 weeks.

How long will my project take?

This depends on complexity:

  • Minor repairs: 1–3 days

  • Deck rebuilds: 3–7 days

  • Kitchen remodels: 2–3 weeks

  • ADU builds: 1.5–3 months
    We provide detailed timelines during the quote process.

Will you help me stay within my budget?

Yes. We offer tiered pricing options and value-engineered alternatives to make your project affordable without compromising quality. Ask us how we can reduce costs.

What kind of materials do you use?

We use high-grade, weather-tested, and code-approved materials, including Trex decking, pressure-treated lumber, composite siding, and premium waterproofing membranes. All material choices are discussed with the client.

Do your projects come with a warranty?

Yes. We offer a 1-year workmanship warranty on all projects and pass on manufacturer warranties where applicable.

Are you licensed and insured?

Yes. BRE Builders is fully licensed, bonded, and insured in Nevada and California. License numbers available upon request.

Can you provide references or photos of past work?

Of course. Visit our portfolio or contact us for direct client referrals.

What payment methods do you accept?

We accept checks, bank transfers, and credit card payments. A deposit is required upon contract signing, with milestone-based payments throughout.

Do you offer financing options?

We are working with third-party lenders to provide flexible home improvement financing. Ask about current availability when requesting your quote.

Can I legally build an ADU in Reno or Carson City?

Yes — but there are zoning, lot-size, and occupancy rules. We help homeowners navigate all ADU permits and designs based on your property’s zoning laws. Read more in our ADU guide.

How to Add an ADU in Nevada Without Breaking the Bank (2025 Guide)

May 26, 2025 Structural Repairs

We Specialize In ADU Design Garage Conversions In-Law Units Guest Cottages Affordable Backyard Builds

Unlock the potential of your property with a cost-effective Accessory Dwelling Unit.

What Is an ADU?

An Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) is a secondary housing unit on a single-family residential lot. ADUs can be:

Detached:

A separate structure from the main house.

Attached:

An addition to the existing home.

Garage Conversion:

Transforming an existing garage into a living space.

ADUs offer flexible living solutions, including housing for family members, rental income opportunities, and increased property value.

Why Build an ADU in Reno?

Affordable Housing Solution:

ADUs provide a cost-effective way to add housing in high-demand areas.

Rental Income:

Generate additional income by renting out the unit.

Multigenerational Living:

Accommodate family members while maintaining privacy.

Increased Property Value:

Enhance the overall value of your property.

2025 Reno ADU Regulations

As of 2025, Reno has updated its zoning codes to support the development of ADUs:

For detailed regulations, refer to the City of Reno’s Zoning Code.

Zoning:

ADUs are permitted in most residential zones.

Size Restrictions:

The maximum size for a detached ADU is 1,200 square feet or 50% of the primary dwelling’s size, whichever is less.

Setbacks:

ADUs must comply with standard setback requirements.

Parking:

One additional parking space is required for the ADU, unless exemptions apply.

Owner Occupancy:

Either the primary residence or the ADU must be owner-occupied.

Financing Your ADU

Building an ADU is a significant investment. Here are some financing options:

Home Equity Loan or HELOC

Leverage the equity in your home to fund the construction of your ADU.

  • Pros: Lower interest rates, potential tax benefits.

  • Cons: Your home serves as collateral.

Construction Loan

A short-term loan specifically for building projects, which converts to a mortgage upon completion.

  • Pros: Designed for construction, interest-only payments during the build.

  • Cons: Requires detailed plans and budget.

ADU Grants and Incentives

Explore local and state programs offering financial assistance:

  • Nevada Housing Grants: Funds available for sustainable home improvements, including ADUs. Learn more.

  • CalHFA ADU Grant Program: Offers up to $40,000 for pre-development costs. Details here.

Designing an Affordable ADU

To keep costs manageable:

Simplify Design:

Opt for straightforward layouts to reduce architectural and construction expenses.

Use Existing Structures:

Converting a garage or basement can be more cost-effective than building anew.

Standardize Materials:

Using common materials can lower costs and simplify construction.

Frequently asked questions

How long does it take to build an ADU?

The timeline varies but typically ranges from 6 to 12 months, including design, permitting, and construction.

Can I rent out my ADU?

Yes, ADUs can be rented, providing a source of income. Ensure compliance with local rental regulations.

Do I need a separate utility connection for the ADU?

It depends on local requirements. Some jurisdictions allow shared utilities; others may require separate connections

BRE Builders: Your ADU Partner in Reno

At BRE Builders, we specialize in:

Custom ADU Design:

Tailored solutions to fit your needs and budget.

Navigating Permits:

Assistance with zoning, permits, and compliance.

Quality Construction:

Delivering durable and efficient ADUs.

Contact us today to start your ADU project. Get a Quote

5 Signs It’s Time to Remodel Your Kitchen – #2 Hurts Your Home Value

May 22, 2025 Kitchen Remodeling

We Specialize In Kitchen Layout Fixes Outdated Kitchen Upgrades Cabinet & Fixture Replacement Smart Storage Solutions High-ROI Kitchen Remodeling

Your kitchen is more than just a place to cook—it’s central to your home’s function and value. When signs of wear and inefficiency show up, ignoring them costs you comfort and money. Below are five key signals it’s time to remodel—each with direct impact on usability and resale. If any of these sound familiar, it’s time to consult with Reno’s kitchen remodeling experts at BRE Builders.

You Avoid Cooking Because the Layout Is a Nightmare

Every time you cook, it feels like a fight—refrigerator door blocks your oven, prep space is nonexistent, and someone always has to leave the room just so the other can reach the sink.

What It Means:
Your kitchen’s layout is outdated and inefficient. It wasn’t designed for how people cook and live today—especially if multiple people use it at once.

Why It’s Dangerous:
Bad layouts waste time, cause accidents, and kill the joy of cooking. Worse, buyers notice instantly and see it as a major fix, which lowers your resale value.

You’re Embarrassed to Have Guests in Your Kitchen

You find yourself apologizing for peeling cabinets, cracked tiles, or an old stove. You avoid hosting—even though you love company—because your kitchen looks stuck in a past decade.

What It Means:
Your finishes, cabinets, and appliances are visibly worn or outdated. This signals long-term neglect, even if everything technically “works.”

Why It’s Dangerous:
Outdated kitchens lower home value fast. Buyers see an expensive project, not potential. You’re also living every day in a space you no longer enjoy.

Your Counters Are Covered Because There’s Nowhere Else to Put Anything

You’re constantly moving stuff around just to find room to chop vegetables. Your cabinets are packed, and you’ve started using the top of the fridge as storage.

What It Means:
You have a serious storage problem. Your kitchen doesn’t include modern features like pull-out shelves, deep drawers, or smart pantry space.

Why It’s Dangerous:
Lack of storage creates daily chaos and frustration. Cluttered kitchens look smaller, feel stressful, and signal poor design to potential buyers.

Something Smells Off—and You’re Not Sure Why

That musty smell under the sink, the soft spot near the dishwasher, or the discolored wall behind the stove—those aren’t just quirks. They’re warnings.

What It Means:
You likely have hidden water damage or mold. Leaks have affected your cabinets, subfloor, or drywall—and it’s spreading quietly.

Why It’s Dangerous:
Ignoring damage risks rot, health hazards, and major repairs later. Remodeling lets you address the problem, not just cover it up.

Your Power Bill Keeps Going Up and Your Kitchen Stays Cold

The lights are dim, the oven takes forever to preheat, and your kitchen is freezing in the winter—yet your bill is sky high. Something doesn’t add up.

What It Means:
Your appliances are outdated, your lighting is inefficient, and your kitchen likely lacks proper insulation or modern HVAC flow.

Why It’s Dangerous:
You’re wasting money every month and living in discomfort. Energy-inefficient kitchens are expensive to run and unattractive to buyers.

Why Reno Homeowners Choose BRE Builders

Local experts who understand Nevada homes
Custom remodeling—no cookie-cutter designs
Clear timelines, clean job sites, and real communication
5-star rated service with lasting results

8 Signs Your Deck Is No Longer Safe – #5 Can Be Fatal

May 22, 2025 Deck Repair

We Specialize In Deck Safety Inspections Deck Rot Repair Deck Rebuilding in Reno Waterproofing & Finishing Custom Railing Installation

Soft or Spongy Wood Underfoot

If your deck feels soft, bouncy, or uneven when you walk on it, that’s a sign of hidden wood rot or moisture intrusion.

  • What it means: The deck boards or substructure are decaying from within.

  • Why it’s dangerous: It reduces load capacity and increases the risk of collapse.

Wobbly or Loose Railings

A secure railing is essential for safety—especially on elevated decks. If your railing sways or detaches with minimal pressure, you’re at risk.

  • Cause: Corroded fasteners or decaying wood anchors.

  • Fix it fast: This is a serious fall hazard.

Cracked or Splintering Boards

Cracks and splinters aren’t just cosmetic. They indicate drying, stress fractures, or UV damage that weakens the deck.

  • Common in: Old cedar, redwood, or untreated pine decks.

  • Watch for: Longitudinal splits or boards that flex under pressure.

Rusty or Missing Fasteners

Look underneath your deck. If you see rusted nails, loose bolts, or missing brackets, that means your framing is compromised.

  • Risk: Structural failure.

  • Inspection tip: Also check joist hangers and support brackets.

Sinking or Tilting Footings

(This One Can Be Fatal)

Deck footings bear the entire weight of the structure. When they tilt or sink, the deck can collapse suddenly—especially during storms or heavy loads.

  • Cause: Poor soil conditions, erosion, or improper concrete installation.

  • Urgency: This issue requires immediate repair.

Black Stains, Mold, or Algae Growth

Discoloration or black patches often signal excess moisture retention. Mold doesn’t just damage your deck—it affects your health.

  • Typical areas: Under planters or in shaded corners.

  • Remedy: Improve drainage and waterproofing.

Hollow-Sounding or Insect-Damaged Wood

If parts of your deck sound hollow when tapped or you notice sawdust trails, you may have a termite or carpenter ant infestation.

  • Warning signs: Small holes, soft spots, sagging boards.

  • Act fast: Infestation spreads quickly in untreated wood.

Peeling Paint or Flaking Sealant

If your deck’s paint is peeling or the sealant is cracking, the wood is likely absorbing water—speeding up rot, mold, and UV damage.

  • Maintenance tip: Re-seal every 2–3 years, especially in snow-prone areas like Tahoe.

  • Visual clue: Grayed or splintered wood surface.

Top 10 Signs Your Home Needs Structural Repair – Don’t Ignore #3

Your home might look fine on the surface, but underneath? Serious structural issues could be…

Top 10 Signs Your Home Needs Structural Repair – Don’t Ignore #3

May 22, 2025 Structural Repairs

Is Your Home Showing Signs Of Wall Cracks? Sagging Floors? Sticking Doors? Dry Rot? Foundation Shifts? Water Damage?

Your home might look fine on the surface, but underneath? Serious structural issues could be forming. Small cracks and subtle shifts often go unnoticed until they become expensive problems.

At BRE Builders, we’ve seen firsthand how early warning signs can save homeowners thousands — if caught in time.

Cracks in Walls or Ceilings (Especially at Corners)

Long, diagonal cracks or those near windows and doors can mean your home is shifting or settling improperly.

Uneven or Sloping Floors

If a marble rolls across your floor unprovoked, that’s not a parlor trick — it’s a red flag. Sloping floors often indicate failing joists or foundation issues.

Doors & Windows That Stick or Won’t Close Properly

This is one of the earliest and most ignored signs. As framing shifts, door and window openings become distorted. If multiple start sticking at once, don’t ignore it.

Bowed or Bulging Walls

Walls that bow outward (or inward) could indicate significant structural stress or load imbalance.

Cracks in Foundation or Basement Walls

Wide or horizontal cracks in concrete aren’t cosmetic — they’re signs of foundational movement or hydrostatic pressure.

Sagging Roofline or Gutter Separation

A drooping roofline often points to truss or beam failure. Detached gutters may mean water is damaging structural elements below.

Gaps Around Window Frames or Exterior Doors

If you can see light through your window frame — your house is shifting. These gaps also affect insulation and increase utility costs.

Musty Smells or Persistent Dampness

Damp areas under floors or around foundations can lead to rot — a structural killer. Learn more about dry rot removal here.

Cracking Tile or Countertops

Tiles or granite surfaces shouldn’t shift or crack on their own. Subtle structural motion causes this.

Structural “Repairs” Done by Previous Contractors

Quick fixes or hidden patch jobs often mean you’ve inherited someone else’s shortcuts. We’ve fixed plenty — the right way.

Office Space Remodel

July 5, 2023

Modern Office Space Remodel – Built to Reflect Business Efficiency

Complete tenant improvement project in Reno, NV for a leading financial institution, designed and built from the ground up by BRE Builders.

Project Scope & Summary

BRE Builders completed a full office remodel in Reno, NV for a national financial institution. The client required a total demolition of the existing space and a fully redesigned layout that aligned with their strict architectural and branding standards. We managed all stages: framing, electrical, mechanical, millwork, and finish—resulting in a functional, modern office equipped for productivity and comfort. 📍 Location: Reno, NV

Construction Phase

Infrastructure + Interior

Final Finish

What This Remodel Included

Full demolition and new layout
Electrical, plumbing, HVAC upgrades
Custom cabinetry and millwork
ADA-compliant restrooms
Energy-efficient lighting
Breakroom with appliances
IT/data room buildout
Conference rooms with A/V setup

The Blue Reef Way.

Licensced since 1989, we have been the areas go-to General Contractor for any and all residential construction needs. We take pride in helping our clients reach their construction goals. We are excited to be part of any build; helping a family see their custom dream home come to life, supporting a property flipper reach high value ROI, enhancing a home with a kitchen, bath, or basement remodel, adding additional value to a homeowner with an ADU, loft/condominium remodels, and updating a homes with additions or decks.

People first

Our foundation is built by treating all people with dignity and respect.

History & legacy

Focus on the values our company was built on and honor them.

Lead by example

Commitment to being held to the highest standards.

Tell Us About Your Project

Innovation

Do not be complacent. Be industry innovators.

Together We Win

Pick the right partners. Be a good partner.

Testimonials. 

Testimonials. 

Testimonials. 

Testimonials. 

Testimonials. 

“

I have worked with Steve for years. Always able to work with my budget and deliver beyond expectations. My go-to local expert.

MattRocklin, CA
“

The team was quick to respond to my request. Hard to find reliable people and I trust them.

JennSacramento, CA
“

The team at BRE Builders exceeds my expectations every time I have used them. I have had their team work on several of my projects and they have always met their stated deadlines and come in at bid. I continue to use them for large and small projects and recommend them whenever I can.

CarmelReno, NV
“

Steve and Chris and the whole team at BRE Builders have been a joy to work with. I can always count on them to get the job done and the results have been fabulous!

BillSparks, NV
“

I am so glad that I found these guys, their team was able to meet all of my needs! Concrete, new deck, kitchen remodel, electrical and even landscaping! What a relief it was to and one company that could handle all of my needs under one roof. Highly recommend!

AustinReno, NV
“

Steve and his team at Blue Reef are jacks of all trades and licensed in both Nevada and California which has been great for me as I have several investment properties in both states. It’s the company to call if you have friends or family in Reno/Tahoe or the other side of the border in California.

ReggieModesto, CA

Our Commitment to Quality, Value & Trust

We deliver exceptional service backed by a focus on quality, fair pricing, and a reliable warranty — ensuring lasting partnerships built on trust.

Exceptional Service

We go above and beyond to ensure every client is satisfied, offering prompt support, expert guidance, and a seamless experience from start to finish.

Quality Focused

Our work speaks for itself — we use premium tools, follow best practices, and prioritize attention to detail to deliver lasting results.

Competitive Pricing

We offer top-notch services at prices that respect your budget — no hidden fees, just honest value.

Fair Warranty

Our commitment doesn’t end after delivery. Enjoy peace of mind with a transparent, fair warranty that ensures your satisfaction long-term.

commercial services

August 30, 2022

commercial services

TRUST & EXPERTISE

When working a contractor on your commercial property it comes down to two things: trust and expertise. Blue Reef Enterprises prides itself with delivering on these two client needs more than anything else. We have honed our craft for years and know we have the expertise to deliver on your commercial construction needs. Our lengthy list of repeat partners shows how we deliver on trust. Combining our national experience and local expertise, we offer unparalleled service to our clients on new construction and commercial refurbishment projects. Let us help your commercial building goals.

residential services

August 30, 2022

residential services

new homes & remodels

Licensced since 1989, we have been the areas go-to General Contractor for any and all residential construction needs. We take pride in helping our clients reach their construction goals. We are excited to be part of any build; helping a family see their custom dream home come to life, supporting a property flipper reach high value ROI, enhancing a home with a kitchen, bath, or basement remodel, adding additional value to a homeowner with an ADU, loft/condominium remodels, and updating a homes with additions or decks.

We go above and beyond to ensure every client is satisfied, offering prompt support, expert guidance, and a seamless experience from start to finish.

Quality Focused

Our work speaks for itself — we use premium tools, follow best practices, and prioritize attention to detail to deliver lasting results.

Competitive Pricing

We offer top-notch services at prices that respect your budget — no hidden fees, just honest value.

Fair Warranty

Our commitment doesn’t end after delivery. Enjoy peace of mind with a transparent, fair warranty that ensures your satisfaction long-term.

“

I have worked with Steve for years. Always able to work with my budget and deliver beyond expectations. My go-to local expert.

MattRocklin, CA

The team was quick to respond to my request. Hard to find reliable people and I trust them.

JennSacramento, CA
“

The team at BRE Builders exceeds my expectations every time I have used them. I have had their team work on several of my projects and they have always met their stated deadlines and come in at bid. I continue to use them for large and small projects and recommend them whenever I can.

Steve and Chris and the whole team at BRE Builders have been a joy to work with. I can always count on them to get the job done and the results have been fabulous!

“

I am so glad that I found these guys, their team was able to meet all of my needs! Concrete, new deck, kitchen remodel, electrical and even landscaping! What a relief it was to and one company that could handle all of my needs under one roof. Highly recommend!

“

Steve and his team at Blue Reef are jacks of all trades and licensed in both Nevada and California which has been great for me as I have several investment properties in both states. It’s the company to call if you have friends or family in Reno/Tahoe or the other side of the border in California.

ReggieModesto, CA

Home

November 14, 2021

From Design to Finish

At Blue Reef Builders, We Build With Precision Integrity Strength Vision Craftsmanship Innovation Detail Passion Trust Excellence

Menu
  • About
  • Services
  • Projects
  • Values
  • Testimonials

About BRE Builders

With years of experience, Blue Reef Enterprises, LLC is a licensed general contractor committed to delivering exceptional craftsmanship in home remodeling, custom home building, and commercial tenant improvements, as well as other applications. Our focus is and always will be on quality, innovation, and above all, customer satisfaction. It is what sets us apart in the industry.

Licenses

Licensed in Nevada and California

NV Lic# 85999

CA Lic# 1093798

30+

Years of experience

Our Services. 

Our Services. 

Our Services. 

Our Services. 

Our Services. 

Other
Home Remodeling

Home Remodeling

From kitchens to bathrooms, and decks to roofing, we take your ideas and turn them into reality. We take your vision, create a set of plans, and then, bring it to life.
Landscape & Hardscape

Landscape & Hardscape

You need a deck, a patio cover, an outdoor kitchen, pavers, a retaining wall, whatever you have in mind we can do the job.
Custom Home Building

Custom Home Building

Build your dream home with Blue Reef Builders—trusted custom home builders in Northern Nevada, and Northern California. Personalized in house design, expert craftsmanship, and full project management.
Commercial Tenant Improvements

Commercial Tenant Improvements

Tailored solutions for your business space upgrades. We have experience in all types of commercial construction including building ground up buildings, parking lot work, concrete stairs and walkways, electrical upgrades, and everything in between.

Precision, Purpose, Integrity, Innovation, Strength 

Precision, Purpose, Integrity, Innovation, Strength

With years of experience, Blue Reef Enterprises, LLC is a licensed general contractor committed to delivering exceptional craftsmanship in home remodeling, custom home building, and commercial tenant improvements, as well as other applications.
